name: Monterrubio de la Serena - Laguna de los Lobos Loop
name_original: Monterrubio de la Serena — circular por la Laguna Lobos
description: Hike the moderate 7.9-mile Monterrubio de la Serena circular trail by Laguna Lobos, gaining 1122 feet in about 3 hours and 42 minutes.
region_intro: Hiking around Monterrubio De La Serena offers routes through a landscape characterized by rolling hills, botanical areas, and views of the Embalse de la Serena reservoir. The region features varied terrain, including paths near historical castles and natural reserves. These trails provide opportunities to explore the local flora and expansive water features. Best hiking trails around Monterrubio De La Serena The most popular hiking route is Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail (SL-BA 198), a 2.9 miles (4.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete, featuring diverse plant life. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mirador Celeste de Galizuela from Puebla de Alcocer – round trip via the Castillo and the Embalse de la Serena, a moderate 8.7 miles (14.0 km) path. This route offers views of the castle and the large reservoir. Local hikers also love the Benquerencia Castle Trail, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through historical areas and local hills, often completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes. Hiking around Monterrubio De La Serena is defined by botanical trails, reservoir views, and paths near historical castles. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Monterrubio De La Serena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a small number of reviews. Nearly 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Monterrubio De La Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Monterrubio De La Serena?
A: There are 14 hiking routes around Monterrubio De La Serena, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks. These trails explore the region's rolling hills, botanical areas, and reservoir views.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Monterrubio De La Serena?
A: The terrain around Monterrubio De La Serena is varied, featuring rolling hills, paths near historical castles, and routes through natural reserves. You'll encounter diverse plant life and expansive views, particularly of the Embalse de la Serena reservoir.

Q: Are there easy hiking routes suitable for beginners in Monterrubio De La Serena?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy hiking routes in the area. One excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293234766/benquerencia-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benquerencia Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.5 miles (5.7 km) long and leads through historical areas and local hills, typically completed in about 1 hour 32 minutes.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, there are 2 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991604893/cerro-masatrigo-botanical-trail-sl-ba-198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail (SL-BA 198)&lt;/a&gt;, despite its shorter distance, is rated difficult due to its terrain and elevation changes, offering diverse plant life over 2.9 miles (4.6 km).

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Monterrubio De La Serena?
A: Yes, you can find several circular routes. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101692844/mirador-celeste-de-galizuela-from-puebla-de-alcocer-round-trip-via-the-castillo-and-the-embalse-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Celeste de Galizuela from Puebla de Alcocer – round trip via the Castillo and the Embalse de la Serena&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.7-mile (14.0 km) path offering views of the castle and the large reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199599630/el-zurquillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Zurquillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Many trails offer scenic views and points of interest. You can explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Dam&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Masatrigo Mountain and La Serena Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Celeste de Galizuela&lt;/a&gt; also provides stunning panoramic vistas.

Q: Are the hiking trails around Monterrubio De La Serena suitable for families?
A: Many of the easier routes are suitable for families. Trails that are shorter and have less elevation gain, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293234766/benquerencia-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benquerencia Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt;, are good choices for outings with children, allowing them to explore historical areas and natural landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Monterrubio De La Serena?
A: Spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for hiking in this region. The temperatures are milder, and the landscape is vibrant with flora. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable if visiting during that time.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Monterrubio De La Serena?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in natural areas, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails near Monterrubio De La Serena?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting directly from Monterrubio De La Serena or nearby villages like Puebla de Alcocer, you can often find street parking or designated small parking areas near the trailheads. It's recommended to check the specific tour details on komoot for parking suggestions.

Q: Are there any trails that feature historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293234766/benquerencia-castle-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benquerencia Castle Trail&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads through historical areas and offers views of local hills. Other routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101692844/mirador-celeste-de-galizuela-from-puebla-de-alcocer-round-trip-via-the-castillo-and-the-embalse-de-la-serena" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador Celeste de Galizuela from Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;, also provide views of castles and historical landmarks.

Q: What should I bring for a hike in Monterrubio De La Serena?
A: For hiking in Monterrubio De La Serena, essential items include comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are recommended, and snacks are always a good idea for longer routes.
tips: Monterrubio de la Serena is a small town on the Platou de la Serena in the foothills of the Sierra de Oro. A great natural outdoor hiking landscape to the southwest of the town.

The Wolf Lagoon, as the myth goes, is a rock formation where water is collected during the rainy season and is believed to provide shelter and water to wild wolves. The place is located along the hiking trails outside Monterrubio de la Serena in the Sierra de Oro.
Gravel hiking trail on the outskirts of the town of Monterrubio, surrounded by olive plantations, the mountain range above is in sight.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Monterrubio de la Serena — circular by the Laguna Lobos trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hiking route is estimated to take around 3 hours and 42 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace, stops for photos, and breaks.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: The Monterrubio de la Serena — circular by the Laguna Lobos trail is classified as a moderate hike. This means it's suitable for most regular hikers, but you should expect some elevation changes and potentially uneven terrain.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: While specific trailhead parking details are not extensively documented, it's generally advisable to look for parking within Monterrubio de la Serena, especially near the starting point of the circular route. Many trails in rural areas begin near village centers or accessible roads.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: Based on its moderate difficulty and location, you can anticipate a mix of paths, possibly including dirt tracks, gravel sections, and natural trails. While detailed descriptions are scarce, it's typical for hikes in this region to feature varied landscapes, potentially including open fields and areas around the lake.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Monterrubio de la Serena — circular by the Laguna Lobos trail?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter a few notable spots. These include the historic Church of San Mateo in Monterrubio de la Serena, the scenic La Laguna del Lobo, and sections of the Monterrubio Trail itself.

faqs.question: Is the Monterrubio de la Serena — circular by the Laguna Lobos trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially around natural areas like La Laguna del Lobo, to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please also remember to clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, typically, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to hike the Monterrubio de la Serena — circular by the Laguna Lobos trail. It's generally open for public access.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike in Extremadura, where Monterrubio de la Serena is located, is usually during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for hiking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route intersect with other paths. You will find it passes through parts of the Camino Mozárabe, the Camino de la Laguna de los Lobos, the Camino de Zalamea a Monterrubio de la Serena, and the Camino del Matadero.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Monterrubio de la Serena, you should be able to find local amenities such as cafes, restaurants, or small shops within the town before or after your hike. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate hike like this?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike of this length, we recommend bringing comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a charged phone. Depending on the season, a light jacket or rain gear might also be useful.
